name: Mushrooms in White Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1 tbsp Corn starch dissolved in 1/4 cup Water
recipeInstructions:
1. Select mushrooms with unopened caps. Cut off the stems of the mushrooms, leaving only the caps. Save the mushroom stems for soup. Mince the slice of ham and mince parsley. Gather all ingredients.
2. Heat oil in a wok over low medium heat for about 2 minutes. Add mushrooms to the wok and stir fry them for 1 minute. Add 2 tablespoons water, cover the wok, and gently stir fry mushrooms for approximately 6 minutes, lifting the lid 2 or 3 times during cooking. When mushrooms are cooked, remove them to a serving platter and arrange with caps upright, pouring juices from the wok over them.
3. In the same wok, add 1/4 cup milk. Stir in the corn starch solution thoroughly. Cook over low medium heat until the white sauce thickens. Pour the thickened sauce over mushroom caps, garnish with minced ham and parsley.
